weakness_analysis: Massive regression from v2's 0.82. Root cause: gemma4:e4b is ignoring the required output template entirely when scan JSON lacks raw code snippets. All three evaluated articles (uber-go-zap, gofiber-fiber, labstack-echo) are minimal bullet-point summaries with no Analysis by Theme, no Remediation Strategy, no Summary Table, no Severity Assessment, and zero code examples. The v3 change (fix code accuracy rule) is not the cause — the regression is caused by the model collapsing to a summary format when it cannot quote real code. The prompt enforces code citation but does not explicitly state that all template sections are mandatory regardless of scan data quality.
hypothesis: Add explicit 'Template Enforcement (NON-NEGOTIABLE)' section to the prompt that: (1) lists all required sections as mandatory, (2) explicitly instructs that even with metadata-only scans the full template must be produced with callout placeholders where code would go, (3) prohibits bullet-point summary format. This targets the structural collapse behavior that caused the 0.083 aggregate score.
